channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FCT Minister Wike Reflects on Challenges and Support During Local Government Elections
item.description: The Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike, yan say plenty people wan make am fail for the local government elections wey just happen for Abuja. For him monthly media chat, Wike ...

item.title: Kenya’s counties showing progress in job creation and healthcare under devolution
item.description: A recent report by the Kenya Vision 2030 Delivery Secretariat has ranked Murang’a, Nakuru, Kiambu, and Kisumu as the top counties in Kenya for job creation, automation, and e-government services. Mura...

item.title: GPS and Navigation Systems Disrupted for Over 1,100 Ships Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Since the start of the United States and Israel’s military operations against Iran on February 28, 2026, over 1,100 ships in the Middle East have experienced GPS and AIS (Automatic Identification Syst...

item.title: How to set Mail & Guardian as your preferred news source on Google
item.description: Google has introduced a new feature that allows users to personalise their news experience by selecting preferred publications for the 'top stories' section in search results. This means you no longer...

item.title: Trump criticised for aggressive actions in Iran amid UK political reactions
item.description: The article critiques former US President Donald Trump's recent actions following his omission from the Nobel Peace Prize. It highlights Trump's pivot from peace initiatives to military actions, inclu...

item.title: Presidency Raises Concern Over ADC’s Digital Membership Registration Platform
item.description: The Senior Special Assistant on Digital and New Media to President Bola Tinubu, O’tega Ogra, don tok say the new online membership registration system of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) get waha...

item.title: Inside Trump’s Decision to Launch Military Action Against Iran
item.description: In early 2026, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pressed US President Donald Trump for approval to strike Iran’s missile sites. Over months of secret discussions, Trump administration official...

item.title: University of Karachi halts academic activities in solidarity with victims of Iran attacks
item.description: The University of Karachi (KU) has suspended all classes and examinations scheduled for March 2 in order to express solidarity with the victims of the recent attacks in Iran. According to an official ...

item.title: Pakistan Denies Being Next Target Amid US-Israeli Strikes on Iran
item.description: A senior Pakistani security official on Monday categorically rejected rumours that Pakistan could be targeted next following the US and Israeli military strikes on Iran. The official emphasised that s...

item.title: Alberta Premier Links Middle East Tensions to Canadian Pipeline Development
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith has stated that the recent conflict involving Iran reinforces the urgency for Canada to expand its domestic pipeline infrastructure. Speaking amid rising oil prices and ...

item.title: Traffic Disrupted on Mombasa Road After Fuel Tanker and Container Lorry Collision in Athi River
item.description: There was heavy traffic disruption along Mombasa Road in the Athi River area after a fuel tanker collided with a trailer carrying a 40-foot container and burst into flames on Monday morning. The two l...

item.title: President Samia to inaugurate construction of 15 new oil storage tanks at Dar es Salaam Port
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: The President of Tanzania, Samia Suluhu Hassan, is set to officially attend the foundation-laying ceremony for 15 new oil storage tanks at Dar es Salaam Port tomorrow, 3rd March 2026. A...

item.title: US Embassy and Consulates Suspend Services in Pakistan Amid Protests
item.description: The US Mission to Pakistan has cancelled all visa and American Citizen Services (ACS) appointments for March 2 at its Embassy in Islamabad and Consulates in Karachi and Lahore due to security concerns...

item.title: South African Health Department Officials Face Charges of Fraud and Theft
item.description: The Hawks, South Africa’s Directorate for Priority Crime Investigation, has executed arrest warrants against the Health Department’s director-general, Sandile Buthelezi, along with two senior official...

item.title: Australia considers criminalising pro-Palestine phrases without clear context
item.description: Queensland and New South Wales are exploring legislation that could criminalise certain pro-Palestine phrases, such as 'globalise the intifada'. The proposed measures have sparked debate over free spe...

item.title: Pakistan's JF-17 Thunder Gains International Interest, MoU Signed at Dubai Airshow 2025
item.description: Pakistan's JF-17 Thunder has drawn notable attention at the Dubai Airshow 2025, marking a significant achievement for the country's aviation sector. During the event, a memorandum of understanding (Mo...

item.title: President Samia names new ambassadors and reshuffles diplomatic postings
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has carried out a significant reshuffle of Tanzania’s diplomatic corps. Lieutenant General Mathew Mkingule has been appointed as the new ambassador to Cuba, replacing Hum...

item.title: Senate Confirms New Schedule for BARMM Elections in September 2026
item.description: The Philippine Senate has approved a bill to reset the first regular elections in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) to the second Monday of September 2026, with subsequent el...

item.title: Russia and Tanzania Deepen Cooperation in Defence and Education
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania and Russia dey continue to build strong partnership wey don last for more than 60 years, focusing on defence, education, trade, and political cooperation. The Russian Ambassad...

item.title: How the UK’s trade deficit affects household costs and exposure to global shocks
item.description: The UK’s trade deficit in goods reached a record £248.3 billion in 2025, meaning the country spent far more on imports than it earned from exports. While the UK maintains a strong surplus in services ...
